assertions.reason: Both real opencode runs returned final answers and exited normally in about 12 seconds, well inside the bounded-session and post-answer exit limits.
Neither transcript showed visible apology loops, duplicate progress chatter, or empty-turn retry spirals; each run produced a single concise answer block.
Final outputs contained concrete runtime findings and tuning recommendations such as reducing context size, increasing parallelism, and adjusting repeat penalty.

The exact prompt remained anchored on proxy and llama runtime analysis; responses discussed proxy endpoint, upstream URL, llama model, and tuning parameters without drifting into unrelated project areas.
A second fresh session started cleanly and produced a new benchmark answer without stale continuation text or leaked state from the prior run.

A bounded backend-outage run was exercised directly: the CLI surfaced a concise operator-usable connection failure (`Unable to connect. Is the computer able to access the url?`) immediately after a `ConnectionRefused` stream error instead of hanging, looping, or falsely reporting success.
Final user-visible outputs were clean markdown findings with no leaked raw tool payloads, malformed wrappers, or protocol debris.
Each run exited immediately after the final answer with no additional empty/progress-only transcript tail.
Startup logs showed plugin loading without repeated bootstrap exceptions or user-plugin failure spam; startup remained attributable and clean in both runs.
The exact benchmark prompt completed through the real client path and proxy-backed local model, with matching proxy and llama evidence for the same session window.

The rerun evidence still does not prove actual end-to-end tool activity for the benchmark workflow. The client JSONL artifacts show tool availability/resolution only, while proxy evidence still includes `ANALYSIS ROUTE: disabled 37 tools for analysis-only prompt`, so the contract's required transcript plus proxy/client proof of executed tool turns is still missing.

Client transcript, proxy health, port ownership, proxy log, and llama log all align on proxy PID 3292797 at 127.0.0.1:4000 and llama PID 3256399 at 127.0.0.1:8080 using model Qwen3.5-35B-A3B-UD-IQ4_XS.gguf.

frictions: Second run stderr recorded an upstream too_many_requests stream error before still yielding a final answer.
Prompt path still lacks transcript plus proxy/client proof of executed tool turns for the same benchmark session.
summary: Validated the rerun evidence for the real opencode benchmark against the live proxy and llama runtime. Bounded upstream failure behavior now passes via an induced outage artifact, while tool-use preservation still fails because the evidence surface does not prove executed tool turns end-to-end for the same benchmark session.
